game_name: Zork I: The Great Underground Empire

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: exploring the abandoned Great Underground Empire, typing commands like 'north' or 'get lamp'
solving puzzles to progress through passageways and unlock new areas
strategic inventory decisions based on combined weight rather than item count
carrying lantern or light source to avoid being eaten by monster
thief wanders underground stealing items; player can fight, evade, or recover items from thief's treasure room
player must fight or overcome specific enemies in certain locations

element_introduction_order.first_appears: implicit from start, explicit as game progresses
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: collecting all treasures hidden behind puzzles increases score and completion measure

typical_relief_triggers: reaching a safe room with description
collecting light source before entering darkness
typical_tension_triggers: reaching an unsolvable or complex puzzle
encountering antagonists that must be fought
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: player-driven; tension emerges from consequence-based puzzles and resource scarcity rather than time pressure; relief occurs through successful problem-solving and discovery

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: hidden treasures behind puzzles; multiple solutions to some puzzles encourage exploration of alternative approaches
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: dark areas conceal danger (grues); thief's treasure room contains stolen items; puzzles themselves act as spatial gates requiring solution to progress

difficulty_curve.early_game: introduces basic navigation and object interaction in safe context; early puzzles are solvable with straightforward exploration
difficulty_curve.mid_game: introduces darkness mechanics and thief antagonist; puzzles become more complex with interdependencies requiring items from other solved puzzles
difficulty_curve.late_game: treasure collection focus intensifies; late-game puzzles have multiple solutions encouraging creative problem-solving
notable_spikes: first encounter with dark area and grue threat
thief encounters and inventory management pressure
multi-step puzzles requiring items from previously solved puzzles
complex scenarios like the Loud Room with multiple solution paths

notable_level_patterns.description: enclosed location containing a primary puzzle (like dam buttons or maze) that must be solved to progress or access resources
unlit areas requiring light source; failure to obtain light results in grue encounter and death
goal location containing valuable items; thief's treasure room contains stolen possessions
notable_level_patterns.example_level: thief's treasure room; final treasure locations

notable_level_patterns.description: puzzles have interdependencies where solving one puzzle provides items needed for another; encourages non-linear exploration
